raw_consumer_statement:
I was traveling internationally and decided to utilize my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX by Citi. I stayed at a hotel ( independent, not chain or franchise ) and tried to pay for the stay using this card on XX/XX/year>. The amount was {$200.00} ( INR XXXX using the exchange rate on the date of the transaction ). My card was declined - I saw it myself on the machine. My wife decided to use her debit card from a different bank and that transaction went through. A few days later I noticed that the charge still posted to my Citi account. I tried resolving it with the hotel but they kept dodging the issue. I opened a dispute with Citi and submitted all the requested info including the receipt from the transaction on my wife 's card as evidence that the payment happened using a different method. Citi seems to have disregarded all the information and closed the dispute without providing a reason in their official response letter. The letter only said that the evidence was insufficient and did not provide any next steps. I called Citi dispute department the next day and they told me that the PDF document I submitted was illegible. I requested to reopen the dispute so I can resubmit better quality scan. Since that conversation I have additional / alternate evidence that I can submit, but the dispute was never reopened and I have not received any further communication from Citi. 

I also have a bigger concern around the integrity of transactions since this experience. If I can see with my own eyes that the card was declined at the point of sale, and the charge still posts to my account, then how can I trust the whole financial system that's built on the assumption that transactions are processed correctly?
